meta.description: Crosswalk bilateral CGI Simple Lithology <-> OSDU LithologyType reference data. Permite traducao de rotulos litologicos entre os dois padroes, suportando ETL Petrobras de mapas geologicos rotulados em CGI para o data lake OSDU.

match_kinds.closeMatch: Conceito muito proximo, possivelmente diferenca de escopo
match_kinds.broadMatch: OSDU e mais generico (CGI e subclasse do OSDU)
match_kinds.narrowMatch: OSDU e mais especifico (raro neste crosswalk)

meta.notes: OSDU LithologyType e um vocabulario plano (sem hierarquia rica). CGI e uma ontologia OWL com herancas multiplas. Logo, muitos CGI conceitos finos colapsam para um unico OSDU value via broadMatch. Conceitos CGI puramente estruturais (ex: grainSupported, foliated) ou QAPF (campos do diagrama Streckeisen) nao mapeiam para LithologyType e foram omitidos.

osdu_lithology_values.definition: Sedimentary rock composed primarily of sand-sized clastic grains.
Fine-grained, fissile sedimentary rock dominated by clay and silt.
Sedimentary carbonate rock dominated by calcite (CaCO3).
Carbonate rock dominated by the mineral dolomite (CaMg(CO3)2). OSDU uses 'Dolomite' for the rock (dolostone).
Calcareous mudstone, mixture of clay and carbonate.
Clastic sedimentary rock of silt-sized grains.
Fine-grained sedimentary rock of clay and silt without fissility.
Coarse clastic rock of rounded gravel-sized clasts in a finer matrix.
Coarse clastic rock of angular fragments.
Poorly sorted clastic rock with wide grain-size range.
Evaporite rock composed dominantly of halite.
Evaporite rock of hydrated calcium sulfate (CaSO4-2H2O).
Sedimentary rock formed by evaporation of saline water.
Partially decayed organic plant material, precursor to coal.
Aluminum-rich residual rock formed by intense weathering.
Lithified pyroclastic rock dominated by ash-sized fragments.
Unconsolidated clastic sediment of sand-sized grains.
Unconsolidated clastic sediment of silt-sized grains.
Unconsolidated fine-grained sediment of clay-sized particles.
Unconsolidated mixture of clay and silt.
Plutonic rock intermediate between granite and tonalite.
Alkali-feldspar-rich plutonic rock with little quartz.
Plutonic rock with subequal alkali and plagioclase feldspar.
Volcanic rock with quartz and intermediate plagioclase.
Low-to-medium grade foliated metamorphic rock between slate and schist.
Metamorphic rock dominated by amphibole and plagioclase.
High-pressure metamorphic rock with omphacite + garnet.
Foliated rock formed by ductile shear deformation.
Metamorphic rock dominated by serpentine-group minerals.
Quartz-rich plutonic rock with plagioclase, low alkali feldspar.
Plutonic rock dominated by plagioclase feldspar.

mappings.note: OSDU has no impurity qualifier; collapse to Limestone.
CGI uses 'dolostone' for rock; OSDU uses 'Dolomite' for both mineral and rock.
Calcareous carbonate sed rock is essentially limestone family.
Mud-supported carbonate (Dunham); OSDU collapses to Limestone.
Carbonate-rich clastic mudstone is the classic marl.
Dunham wackestone -> Limestone in OSDU.
Unconsolidated carbonate sediment.
Igneous carbonate; not a sedimentary carbonate.
Arenite is sandstone with <15% mud matrix; OSDU has only Sandstone.
AMBIGUITY: CGI separates 'arenite' vs 'generic_sandstone' (incl. wackes); OSDU has single Sandstone bucket.
Organic-rich mudstones typical of source rocks; commonly logged as shale in OSDU.
Diamicton is unconsolidated; OSDU has only the lithified Diamictite.
Critical for Santos Basin pre-salt halite layers.
AMBIGUITY: CGI lumps gypsum+anhydrite; consumer must inspect mineralogy. Default to Anhydrite (more common in deep basins). Map to Gypsum if surface/near-surface.
Includes K/Mg salts (sylvite, carnallite, tachyhydrite) — relevant for Aptian Santos pre-salt.
OSDU lacks a 'Bituminous' value; bituminous is the default Coal rank.
Heterogeneous: includes coal AND oil/gas shale. Confirm context before mapping.
Organic ooze; closest sedimentary equivalent in OSDU is organic-rich shale.
Includes chert, novaculite, porcellanite.
Diatomite, radiolarite -> Chert family in OSDU.
Unconsolidated precursor.
Pelagic ooze; could be carbonate or siliceous. Default Mud.
Tephra is unconsolidated pyroclastic; closest OSDU value is Tuff.
Dolerite/diabase: hypabyssal mafic; OSDU has no Dolerite/Diabase, closest is Gabbro.
Ultramafic; closest in OSDU.
Fine-grained leucocratic granite.
Texture term; composition unspecified.
Critical for Parana flood basalts and offshore SDR sequences.
Albitised basalt.
Foid-bearing alkaline mafic; closest is Basalt.
Feldspathoid-dominated; no specific OSDU value.

mappings.cgi_label_en: ash tuff lapillistone and lapilli tuff
tuff breccia agglomerate or pyroclastic breccia
mappings.note: Pyroclastic breccia; could also map to Tuff.
Cataclasite is brittle fault rock; no OSDU equivalent, closest fault-rock is Mylonite.
Massive granular metamorphic; closest is Granulite.
